displayTitle: No Off Switch: Inside the Gamma Camera

beats.narration: A radioisotope has no off switch — it emits around the clock. Gamma equipment is built to keep that radiation locked away.

payload.title: NO OFF SWITCH
payload.sub: Sealed sources and the devices that carry them.

beats.narration: The pellet sits sealed inside two welded stainless capsules, fastened to a short cable — the pigtail. Handle the cable, never the capsule.

payload.heading: Inside the sealed source
payload.note: HANDLE THE CABLE — NEVER THE CAPSULE

beats.narration: Iridium one ninety-two is the field workhorse for mid-range steel. Cobalt sixty penetrates thick sections. Selenium seventy-five gives high contrast on thin walls.
A depleted uranium shield wraps an S shaped tube, blocking any straight path. To expose, crank the source down the guide tube to the collimator.

payload.heading: Anatomy of the gamma camera
payload.note: S-TUBE: NO STRAIGHT PATH FOR RADIATION

beats.narration: The source is always live. Don't trust the crank, the indicator, or memory. Survey with a calibrated meter after every single exposure.

payload.sub: Hot until the meter says otherwise.
